issues: small sprite size reduces sub-0.5s spottability without arrow aid
white/gray base risks blending into adjacent gray stone tiles
slightly pasted feel — pixel density and shading style diverges marginally from surrounding tileset
reasoning: The asset (warm golden-orange round body, white/gray pedestal base) is placed at the transition between dark-red dungeon tiles and gray stone. Warm hues provide moderate contrast against the cool dark background, and the silhouette is compact and distinct. However, the sprite is small (~40px tall in the scene), making unaided spotting slower than 0.5s. The white base partially mirrors the gray cobblestone area to the right, risking merge at a glance. The overall rendering style is close but not fully native — slight luminosity and anti-aliasing differences give a mild paste-in impression. No hue conflict with the enemy slot indicator or UI buttons. Echoes color language (warm accent on dark ground) is respected, keeping the scene relatively clean. Marginal pass; increasing sprite contrast or adding a subtle shadow ring would push to a solid pass.
